data.title: CWT International Launches Group AI & IT Office to Drive AI-Led Transformation
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CWT International Limited ( (HK:0521) ) has provided an update.

CWT International has completed an organisational restructuring and set up a Group AI & IT Office in June 2026, consolidating its IT functions and AI expertise under a unified technology framework operated via its wholly owned subsidiary Prism AI Studio Pte. Ltd. The new office will drive group-wide AI initiatives, standardise development, and build reusable AI solutions aimed at reducing transformation costs and improving returns on technology investment.

The company has also signed a memorandum of understanding between Prism AI and its freight logistics arm CWT Globelink to develop an AI-driven pricing solution as a flagship pilot project, targeting the automation of manual, high-volume data tasks. Management expects this systematic AI-driven transformation to create differentiated, technology-enabled advantages, reallocate staff to higher-value roles, and potentially enable future commercialisation of its AI tools, enhancing long-term profitability and shareholder value.

More about CWT International Limited

CWT International Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company with diversified global operations, including freight logistics through its CWT Globelink business. The group focuses on leveraging technology and digitalization to enhance operational efficiency, support logistics services, and strengthen its competitive position in global supply chain markets.

data.title: ICO Group Clarifies Proxy Form Error Ahead of 2026 AGM
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

ICO Group Limited ( (HK:1460) ) has provided an announcement.

ICO Group Limited has issued a clarification regarding a clerical error in the English version of its proxy form for the 2026 annual general meeting, scheduled for 28 August 2026, which relates to an ordinary resolution extending the general mandate for directors to allot and issue new shares. The company confirmed that only the wording of the resolution was affected, that the existing proxy forms already lodged remain valid, and that no revised proxy form will be issued, minimizing disruption for shareholders ahead of the meeting.

The clarification underscores the company’s effort to maintain procedural transparency around its plans to renew mandates for share issuance, share repurchases, director re-election and auditor re-appointment at the forthcoming AGM. By promptly addressing the error and affirming the validity of previously submitted proxies, ICO Group aims to reassure investors that corporate governance processes remain orderly and that the AGM can proceed without administrative uncertainty for stakeholders.

More about ICO Group Limited

ICO Group Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the technology and IT services sector, providing solutions that support corporate clients in areas such as systems development and digital transformation. The company is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical governance structure for a mid-sized Hong Kong-listed firm.

data.title: KFM Kingdom Holdings Signs Three-Year Connected Contract Manufacturing Deal
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

The latest announcement is out from KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3816) ).

KFM Kingdom Holdings has entered into a three-year contract manufacturing agreement between its subsidiary KPP(SZ) and PRC-based company HHY, effective from 24 July 2026. Under the deal, KPP(SZ) will manufacture and supply products to HHY, with pricing, quantities, specifications, and delivery details to be negotiated separately.

The arrangement constitutes a continuing connected transaction because HHY is 70% indirectly owned by Jack Sun, son of chairman and executive director Peter Sun, making HHY an associate of a connected person under Hong Kong listing rules. As the relevant transaction ratios exceed 0.1% but remain below 5%, the deal is a non-exempt connected transaction subject to disclosure and annual review, but it does not require independent shareholders’ approval, signaling manageable compliance obligations for the company and clarifying governance treatment for investors.

More about KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd.

KFM Kingdom Holdings Ltd. is a Hong Kong-listed company engaged in contract manufacturing activities through its subsidiaries, including KPP(SZ) in the PRC. The group focuses on producing and supplying industrial products to various customers, operating within the broader manufacturing and supply-chain sector in Asia.

Virtual Mind Holding Company Limited has scheduled an extraordinary general meeting to be held in Hong Kong on 13 August 2026 at 11:00 a.m., providing shareholders with an opportunity to attend and vote on corporate matters. data.title: Qinqin Foodstuffs Issues Profit Warning as Revenue and Margins Slide
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company ( (HK:1583) ) is now available.

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group has warned that its unaudited revenue for the six months ended 30 June 2026 is expected to fall about 8% year-on-year to roughly RMB485 million, with profit attributable to equity shareholders plunging around 73% to 82% to between RMB4 million and RMB6 million. The company cited weaker sales through traditional retail channels amid softer consumer spending, lower sales volumes of jelly and rice wine products that reduced factory utilisation and economies of scale, and a related RMB22 million drop in gross profit and a two-percentage-point decline in gross margin, partly offset by stronger sales to snack food chains and growth in its OEM business, prompting a cautionary note to shareholders and investors as final results are still being finalised.

The company noted that the interim figures are based on preliminary, unaudited management accounts and may be adjusted before the formal results announcement scheduled for mid-August 2026. Management urged shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading the company’s shares pending publication of the finalised interim results, highlighting the significant profitability pressure despite some resilience from newer channels and OEM operations.

More about Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company

Qinqin Foodstuffs Group (Cayman) Company Limited is a Hong Kong-listed food manufacturer focused on snack foods, jelly products and rice wine, serving both traditional retail channels and snack food chains. The group also operates an OEM manufacturing business, positioning it as a contract producer for other brands within the broader packaged food sector.

Average Trading Volume: 53,284

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$702.2M

data.title: China Life revamps profit distribution in connected investment partnership
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an announcement.

China Life Insurance Company Limited has announced a change to the profit distribution mechanism of a connected investment partnership formed with China Life Jiangsu, Jiangsu Fund and China Life Property and Casualty Insurance. The partners plan to sign a supplemental agreement by the end of 2026, shifting from project-based profit allocation to a model based on the overall performance of the partnership, aligning more closely with market practices.

Under the new structure, capital will first be returned to limited partners, then to the general partner, followed by an 8% annualized return hurdle for both before remaining excess income is split 80% to limited partners and 20% to the general partner. The board considers the revised terms fair, commercially reasonable and beneficial to shareholders, noting that several directors with roles in related entities abstained from voting on the resolution to avoid conflicts of interest.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy
with a HK$35.00 price target.

China Life Insurance Company Limited is a leading life insurance provider in China, operating as a joint stock company with limited liability and listed in Hong Kong. The company focuses on life insurance and related financial services, supported by its state-owned controlling shareholder China Life Insurance (Group) Company and its affiliated subsidiaries.

data.title: China Life renews connected insurance sales pact with CLP&C
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Life Insurance Co ( (HK:2628) ) has shared an update.

China Life Insurance Company Limited plans to renew and extend its insurance sales cooperation with affiliate China Life Property & Casualty through a new Insurance Sales Framework Agreement. Under the arrangement, China Life will continue to act as an agent for CLP&C, selling a wide spectrum of property and casualty products, including compulsory, automobile and various commercial and agricultural policies, and collecting premiums in CLP&C’s name.

Because CLP&C is an associate of the controlling shareholder, the renewed arrangement is classified as a continuing connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting, announcement and annual review obligations but not independent shareholder approval. However, as cumulative dealings with the parent group exceed 5% of China Life’s latest audited net assets, the new framework requires shareholder approval under Shanghai Stock Exchange rules and, once approved, will replace the existing agreement from 1 January 2027, providing continuity to the group’s distribution and agency income streams.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2628) stock is a Buy
with a HK$35.00 price target.

China Life Insurance Company Limited is a leading Chinese life insurer listed in Hong Kong, providing a broad range of insurance products and related services. The latest announcement is out from SG Group Holdings Ltd ( (HK:1657) ).

SG Group Holdings Limited, a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1657, operates in the professional services and staffing sector, providing human resources solutions to corporate clients in the region. It is now the target of a change-of-control transaction led by Hong Kong Weiye Software Co., Limited, an entity incorporated in Hong Kong that is pursuing ownership of the company through a regulated takeover process.

The company and Hong Kong Weiye Software have jointly announced the despatch of a composite document detailing an unconditional mandatory cash offer for all SG Group Holdings shares not already held by the offeror and its concert parties. The offer opened on 24 July 2026 and is scheduled to close on 14 August 2026, with the documentation including recommendations from an independent board committee and advice from an independent financial adviser, signalling a formal and closely supervised takeover that could reshape SG Group’s governance, board composition and leadership roles.

More about SG Group Holdings Ltd

SG Group Holdings Limited, incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong under stock code 1657, operates in the professional services and staffing industry. The company focuses on providing human resources solutions and related services to corporate clients, positioning itself within Hong Kong’s broader services sector and subject to local securities and takeover regulations.

Hong Kong Weiye Software Co., Limited, incorporated in Hong Kong, serves as the offeror in the current transaction. Through a mandatory cash offer for all issued shares of SG Group Holdings not already owned or agreed to be acquired by the offeror and its concert parties, Hong Kong Weiye Software is seeking effective control of the listed company within the framework of Hong Kong’s Takeovers Code.

United Company RUSAL has entered into new supplementary electrical grid connection contracts with associates of En+ for its Bratsk Aluminium Smelter and an engineering and construction subsidiary. The agreements formalize the purchase of technological connection services to support power-receiving equipment and development facilities, reflecting the group’s ongoing need for reliable grid access to sustain its core operations.

Wai Kee Holdings Limited ( (HK:0610) ) has provided an update.

Wai Kee Holdings and its subsidiary Build King have moved to expand their construction portfolio through PrimeStone, a wholly owned unit of Build King, agreeing to acquire nearly 100% of several Paul Y. Engineering-linked construction companies that are currently in provisional liquidation. The deal, capped at HK$100 million, will see the target firms retained mainly for their construction licences and “Paul Y.” trademarks, becoming consolidated subsidiaries of Build King once conditions are met, and has been classified as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong listing rules, triggering reporting and announcement obligations but not requiring shareholder approval.

More about Wai Kee Holdings Limited

Wai Kee Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-based construction and infrastructure group, with activities spanning civil engineering, construction services and related businesses. Build King Holdings Limited, its listed subsidiary, focuses on delivering large-scale construction projects and engineering solutions in Hong Kong and the region, serving both public sector and commercial clients.

Average Trading Volume: 228,697

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: HK$713.8M

data.title: Build King to Acquire Paul Y. Construction Units for HK$100 Million
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An announcement from Build King Holdings ( (HK:0240) ) is now available.

Build King Holdings, through its wholly owned subsidiary PrimeStone, has agreed to acquire nearly 100% of several construction companies currently under provisional liquidation, for a maximum consideration of HK$100 million. The target entities, indirectly owned by Paul Y. Engineering Group, will retain only their construction licences and “Paul Y.” trademarks upon completion and will become consolidated subsidiaries of Build King.

The transaction, aggregated under Hong Kong Listing Rules, qualifies as a discloseable transaction for both Build King and its parent Wai Kee, triggering reporting and announcement obligations but not requiring shareholder approval. By acquiring these streamlined entities with key licences and branding but without substantial legacy assets or liabilities, Build King positions itself to expand its construction platform while managing risk, potentially strengthening its competitive standing in Hong Kong’s construction market.

More about Build King Holdings

Build King Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed construction group, operating through subsidiaries such as PrimeStone, and focuses on civil engineering and building works in the region. It is a listed subsidiary of Wai Kee Holdings, also a Bermuda-incorporated company, with both entities subject to the Hong Kong Listing Rules for discloseable transactions.

Average Trading Volume: 287,800

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1.75B
